nodes.x: O clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.
for behold, .and lo, I shall triumph over all my enemies, for the Lord God hath spoken it
O sing unto the Lord a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory.
O bless our God, ye people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard:
Now thanks be unto God, which always causeth us to triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.
For the Lord most high is terrible; he is a great King over all the earth.
He beholdeth all high things: he is a king over all the children of pride.
For the Lord is a great God, and a great King above all gods.
The Lord is great in Zion; and he is high above all the people.
The Lord is high above all nations, and his glory above the heavens.
Who is like unto the Lord our God, who dwelleth on high,
He shall subdue the people under us, and the nations under our feet.
And he will lift up an ensign to the nations from far, and will hiss unto them from the end of the earth; and behold, they shall come with speed swiftly; none shall be weary nor stumble among them.
Yea, he will bring other nations unto them, and he will give unto them power, and he will take away from them the lands of their possessions, and he will cause them to be scattered and smitten.
Attend ye nations to his great command 1 The time is now when men must rise or fall. Proclaim his will — the judgment’s near at hand !
¶ And he will lift up an ensign to the nations from far, and will hiss unto them from the end of the earth: and, behold, they shall come with speed swiftly:
He shall choose our inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.
Yet now hear, O Jacob my servant; and Israel, whom I have chosen:
For the Lord’s portion is his people; Jacob is the lot of his inheritance.

[paragraph continues] 18. For I know that the Lord will choose him to be a people for possession unto Himself, above all peoples that are upon the face of the earth. 2 19. And behold, Isaac my son loveth Esau more than Jacob, but I see that thou truly lovest Jacob.
God is gone up with a shout, the Lord with the sound of a trumpet.
In what place therefore ye hear the sound of the trumpet, resort ye thither unto us: our God shall fight for us.
I was in the Spirit on the Lord’s day, and heard behind me a great voice, as of a trumpet,
And when the voice of the trumpet sounded long, and waxed louder and louder, Moses spake, and God answered him by a voice.
So David and all the house of Israel brought up the ark of the Lord with shouting, and with the sound of the trumpet.
And he shall send his angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.
Praise the Lord; for the Lord is good: sing praises unto his name; for it is pleasant.
Praise ye the Lord: for it is good to sing praises unto our God; for it is pleasant; and praise is comely.
Hear, O ye kings; give ear, O ye princes; I, even I, will sing unto the Lord; I will sing praise to the Lord God of Israel.
I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise unto thee.
I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
For God is the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord, when they hear the words of thy mouth.
Sing unto the Lord; for he hath done excellent things; this is known in all the earth.
Sing unto the Lord; for he hath done excellent things: this is known in all the earth.
Sing unto God, ye kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises unto the Lord; Selah:
God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.
The Lord is King for ever and ever: the heathen are perished out of his land.
And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.

And may the grace of God the Father, whose throne is high in the heavens, and our Lord Jesus Christ, who sitteth on the right hand of his power, until all things shall become subject unto him, be, and abide with you forever. Amen.
So the heathen shall fear the name of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.
The Lord sitteth upon the flood; yea, the Lord sitteth King for ever.
The princes of the people are gathered together, even the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth belong unto God: he is greatly exalted.
¶ And he gathered together all the princes of Israel, with the priests and the Levites.

For they have taken of their daughters for themselves, and for their sons: so that the holy seed have mingled themselves with the people of those lands: yea, the hand of the princes and rulers hath been chief in this trespass.
The Lord God which gathereth the outcasts of Israel saith, Yet will I gather others to him, beside those that are gathered unto him.
Kings of the earth, and all people; princes, and all judges of the earth:
